title: The Villagers Face Wapol
description: On pages 9–12, the villagers face wapol as wapol learns that the Straw Hats are heading for Drum Castle, while Dalton and the villagers face the threat of the old tyrant’s return.
scene: The Villagers Face Wapol. Wapol learns that the Straw Hats are heading for Drum Castle, while Dalton and the villagers face the threat of the old tyrant’s return. The focus is the story beat, character decisions, and dialogue driving this part.

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: Whew! I thought I was a goner for a second there.

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: SwOosh

dialogue.speaker: Kuromarimo
dialogue.text: Ahh! your head hasn't been, fully sutured yet, your majesty!

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: Leave this island at once...

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: This land has no more need for us...

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: Wapol?

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: That's,

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: "Your 'majesty" for you,

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: My subject, Dalton.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: D-Dalton isn't your subject anymore!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Dalton was the only man in the former regime that actually fought for our country!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: He risked his life for us and this land!

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: Hmph...

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: It's a shame you didn't die,

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: While you were still the captain of the Royal Guards! Mahahaha!

dialogue.speaker: Kuromarimo
dialogue.text: You've done well surViving for so long on a country without any doctors.

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: Although drum kingdom,

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: Is renowned for its medical knowledge...

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: That save for 20 doctors employed in the castle's research facility, all the other doctors were exiled from this country...

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: Very few outsiders would know,

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: Oh well, that's just this kingdom's law.

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: How dare you call such an absurdity a law!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: With the only doctors in this country locked up in your castle,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: The people have no choice but to beg to you whenever they become sick.

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: No law should turn a country's citizens into its hostages.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Such things are called crimes!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: said everything you wanted to?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Then it's time for you to die! Kill this former captain of the Royal Guards, men!

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: Because I owe a great debt to your father, the former King,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: I had hoped that you would one day realize the err of your ways but I now see how futile that is!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: It's the power of the Bison model-ushi Ushi fruit!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Agh!

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: Shoot!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: He's dodging all the bullets!

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: I won't go easy on you just because you're my former subor- Dinates...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Fiddle Banff!

summary: This part centers on the villagers face wapol, clarifying the immediate conflict and how it moves the chapter toward its next major turn.
